note: Method-defense probe: does the one-word clamp MANUFACTURE the convergence, or just extract it cleanly? Ten census categories spanning the convergence range (oak ~94% to country ~32%), each asked two ways -- clamped ('... Reply with one word only.') and free (bare, no clamp). If the field's clamped-modal still dominates the free-form replies, the monoculture is a property of what models choose, not of how we ask. Scene id = <category>_<clamp|free>; parse by splitting on the LAST underscore (any_word_clamp -> any_word / clamp).
